channel.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world
channel.description: Get the latest news online at Major Digest, the top online news resource for the most reliable news sources. Stay informed about the world around you!

image.title: Major Digest - Reliable and Comprehensive News Sources: world

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, The Hill

item.title: Brazilian beef and leather companies fall short in tackling deforestation, a study finds
item.description: BRASILIA, Brazil (AP) — The next United Nations climate conference, COP30, will be held in Belem, the capital of an Amazon region where widespread deforestation mainly driven by cattle farming has turned the surrounding rainforest from a vital carbon sink into a significant carbon source.
item.dc:creator: FABIANO MAISONNAVE, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Taiwan demands that China end its military activity in nearby waters
item.description: TAIPEI, Taiwan (AP) — Taiwan demanded Wednesday that China end its ongoing military activity in nearby waters, which it said is unilaterally undermining peace and stability and disrupting international shipping and trade.
item.dc:creator: TAIJING WU and KEN MORITSUGU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: 2 Ukrainian women are pulled alive from rubble hours after Russian missile strike
item.description: KYIV, Ukraine (AP) — Rescue crews working through the night pulled two women from rubble more than seven hours after a Russian missile struck a private medical clinic in a southern Ukraine city, killing eight people and wounding 22 others, authorities said Wednesday.
item.dc:creator: ILLIA NOVIKOV, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Romania's pro-Western parties agree to form a majority government after political turmoil
item.description: BUCHAREST, Romania (AP) — Romania’s pro-European parties agreed Wednesday to form a majority government made up of groupings that were traditionally on opposite sides and shutting out far-right nationalists who made significant gains in the election on Dec. 1.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: Organized crime gangs expanded into a third of cities in Brazil’s Amazon, report finds
item.description: RIO DE JANEIRO (AP) — Criminal gangs are operating in over a third of municipalities in Brazil’s Amazon rainforest driving a boom in violence, according to a report published Wednesday by a prominent nonprofit organization.
item.dc:creator: ELÉONORE HUGHES, Associated Press, PIX 11

item.title: FIFA prepares to give Saudi Arabia its biggest sports win yet as host of the 2034 World Cup
item.description: Saudi Arabia will be confirmed by FIFA as host of the men’s soccer 2034 World Cup on Wednesday, giving the oil-rich kingdom its biggest prize yet for massive spending on global sports driven by Crown Prince Mohammed bin Salman.
item.dc:creator: Graham Dunbar, Associated Press, Local 4

item.title: Rights group demands probe of ex-governor, 2 army officers in Congo for possible humanitarian crimes
item.description: DAKAR, Senegal (AP) — Amnesty International called for an investigation Wednesday into a former governor and two top army officials in Congo for “possible crimes against humanity” during a deadly crackdown on demonstrators last year protesting the U.N. peacekeeping mission in the country.
item.dc:creator: MARK BANCHEREAU, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Mike Huckabee 'commends' Biden support for Israel's self-defense
item.description: Israel said Tuesday it had bombed more than 350 military sites in Syria during the previous 48 hours.
item.dc:creator: Liz Jassin, NewsNation

item.title: Syrians in Germany worried by some politicians' eagerness for them to go home after Assad's fall
item.description: BERLIN (AP) — Nearly a decade after he arrived in Germany from Syria and took a selfie with then-Chancellor Angela Merkel, Anas Modamani has finished his university studies and has a German passport.
item.dc:creator: KIRSTEN GRIESHABER and GEIR MOULSON, Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Ex-defense chief in South Korea tried to kill himself after being arrested over martial law
item.description: South Korea’s previous defense minister was stopped from attempting suicide while in detention over last week’s martial law declaration.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KPRC 2

item.title: US loans Ukraine $20B from seized Russian assets
item.description: The Treasury Department gave Ukraine a $20 billion loan funded by seized Russian assets Tuesday as part of a broader Group of Seven (G7) effort to boost the country's defense. The money will be transmitted to Ukraine through the World Bank, which received the loan from the U.S. on Tuesday morning.
item.dc:creator: Sylvan Lane, The Hill

item.title: 1,500-year-old Ten Commandments tablet set for auction
item.description: The tablet has twenty lines of text but only nine of the commandments, with "Thou shalt not take the name of the Lord in vain" omitted.
item.dc:creator: Patrick Djordjevic, NewsNation

item.title: Venezuela's opposition signals flexible timeline toward long-sought government transition
item.description: MADRID (AP) — Venezuela’s political opposition on Tuesday signaled a shift toward a flexible timeline for a government transition that it had long insisted would come next month when the new presidential term is set to begin.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Collapse of Syria’s Assad regime renews US push to find Austin Tice
item.description: The fall of the Bashar al-Assad and the mass liberation of numerous notorious prisons in Syria has brought renewed hope for the location and return of American Austin Tice, held captive for 12 years.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Belarus has dozens of Russian nuclear weapons and is ready for its newest missile, its leader says
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — Belarus' authoritarian President Alexander Lukashenko said Tuesday his country is hosting dozens of Russian nuclear weapons and will prepare facilities for the planned deployment of Moscow's newest hypersonic ballistic missile.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Israel strikes targets in Syria after Assad regime falls
item.description: Israeli forces carried out airstrikes on targets in Syria as Israel continues to take more aggressive action in the country following the collapse of Bashar Assad's regime over the weekend.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Negotiations over the war in Ukraine could start 'in the winter,' Poland's leader Tusk says
item.description: WARSAW, Poland (AP) — Poland’s prime minister said Tuesday that negotiations over Russia's war in neighboring Ukraine could start “in the winter,” as his country prepares to take over the rotating presidency of the European Union on Jan. 1.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Russian lawmakers advance a bill removing the terrorist designation for the Taliban
item.description: MOSCOW (AP) — The lower house of the Russian parliament gave initial approval Tuesday to a long-discussed bill that would set the stage for Moscow to remove the designation of the Taliban in Afghanistan as a terrorist group.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, KGET 17, NBC

item.title: Syria's unaccounted for chemical weapons 'extremely worrying,' UN official says
item.description: Syrian dictator Bashar al-Assad was finally toppled after 13 years of civil war, having ruled Syria since 2000. The world's attention turns to Assad's unaccounted stockpile of chemical weapons.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Syria turmoil puts US troops in bind, scrambles alliances
item.description: The collapse of Bashar Assad’s regime in Syria throws a major wrench into the counter-ISIS mission for the roughly 900 U.S. troops in the country, while also complicating Washington's allies in the region. The U.S.
item.dc:creator: Brad Dress, The Hill

item.title: Here's how the US is countering the Islamic State group during Syria's upheaval
item.description: The U.S. has had troops in Syria for the last decade to battle IS. The tumult following a rebel offensive that toppled Assad has raised fears of an Islamic State resurgence.
item.dc:creator: LOLITA C. BALDOR, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: South Korea's Justice Ministry imposes a travel ban on President Yoon over martial law
item.description: SEOUL, South Korea (AP) — South Korea’s Justice Ministry on Monday imposed an overseas travel ban on President Yoon Suk Yeol as authorities investigate allegations of rebellion and other charges in connection with his short-lived declaration of martial law last week.
item.dc:creator: HYUNG-JIN KIM and KIM TONG-HYUNG, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Hungary's Orbán meets with Trump and Musk
item.description: Hungarian Prime Minister Viktor Orbán met Monday with President-elect Trump, his incoming national security adviser and billionaire Elon Musk, who has wielded significant influence in Trump's orbit. "USA today. The future has begun!" Orbán posted on the social platform X.
item.dc:creator: Brett Samuels, The Hill

item.title: The US believes journalist Austin Tice is alive after disappearing in Syria in 2012, Biden says
item.description: WASHINGTON (AP) — President Joe Biden said Sunday that the U.S. government believes missing American journalist Austin Tice, who disappeared 12 years ago near the Syrian capital, is alive and that Washington is committed to bringing him home after Bashar Assad’s ouster from power in Damascus.
item.dc:creator: ERIC TUCKER and AAMER MADHANI, Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian community in Chicago celebrates end of Assad regime
item.description: It was a sea of green flags on Michigan Avenue Sunday as hundreds of Chicagoans celebrated the end of the regime in Syria.
item.dc:creator: Emmy Victor, NewsNation

item.title: Netanyahu to testify in corruption trial amid multiple conflicts
item.description: Israeli Prime Minister Benjamin Netanyahu will begin his witness testimony Tuesday for his ongoing trial on corruption and fraud charges.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Fall of Syria's Bashar Assad is strategic blow to Iran and Russia, experts say
item.description: Analysts and commentators say the collapse of Syria's Bashar al-Assad's regime is likely to reshape the power dynamics in the Middle East while signaling a critical shift in influence among world powers.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: South Korea imposes a travel ban on President Yoon over martial law declaration
item.description: The South Korean Justice Ministry imposed an overseas travel ban against President Yoon Suk Yeol on Monday over his martial law declaration last week.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Trump says Russia abandoned Syria's Assad, never should have been involved
item.description: President-elect Donald Trump said on Sunday that Russia's abandonment of Syrian President Bashar al-Assad led to his downfall.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: US will remain in eastern Syria and seek to prevent IS resurgence, Pentagon says
item.description: Hours after Syrian rebels announced they had toppled Bashar al-Assad's government, the U.S. deputy assistant secretary of defense for the Middle East said the U.S. will remain in eastern Syria to prevent an ISIS resurgence.
item.dc:creator: Reuters, NewsNation

item.title: Syrian insurgents reach gates of Damascus, threatening decades-long Assad regime
item.description: Syrian insurgents have reached the gates of the nation's capital, shortly after Bashar al-Assad's government abandoned the key city of Homs.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: The Latest: The famed Notre Dame Cathedral in Paris reopens to great fanfare
item.description: PARIS (AP) — Notre Dame is reopening its doors for the first time since a fire in 2019 nearly destroyed Paris' beloved 12th-century cathedral.
item.dc:creator: Associated Press, NewsNation

item.title: Notre Dame Cathedral reopens its doors for first time since devastating 2019 fire
item.description: France's iconic Notre Dame Cathedral is formally reopening its doors on Saturday for the first time since a devastating fire nearly destroyed the 861-year-old landmark in 2019.
item.dc:creator: Thomas Adamson, WFLA, NBC

item.title: South Korean president apologizes for declaring martial law ahead of impeachment vote
item.description: South Korean President Yoon Suk Yeol apologized for declaring martial law earlier in the week, as he now prepares for a parliamentary vote on his impeachment.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: Italian nun among 25 arrested in raid against 'Ndrangheta mafia
item.description: As part of an investigation into the ‘Ndrangheta mafia in the city of Brescia, Italian police have arrested 25 people including a nun known for her prison work.
item.dc:creator: Fox News

item.title: United Kingdom cracks down on junk food TV advertisements
item.description: The United Kingdom's Department of Health estimates that 7.2 billion calories will be removed from the diets of British children.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Prann, NewsNation
